title: Warmed Moroccan Corn Tagine
description: A briny, shattering brunch‑mood Moroccan tagine featuring yellow masarepa, corn kernels, and a fresh papillote finish, all cooked on a robata‑grill for a nut‑free-friendly delight.
instructions: Preheat the robata grill to medium‑high heat.
In a bowl whisk together yellow masarepa, vegetable broth, tomato paste, harissa paste, lemon zest, and a pinch of salt until a thick, spiced mixture forms.
Toss the corn kernels, diced bell pepper, chopped onion, and minced garlic with olive oil and a dash of pepper, then spread them evenly in a foil parchment papillote.
Place the prepared papillote on the preheated robata grill, close the grill lid, and cook for 8 minutes, shaking the grill halfway to ensure even heat.
Remove the papillote and carefully open it, letting the steam escape, then stir the contents into the masarepa mixture.
Return the mixture to the grill, cover, and cook for an additional 4 minutes until the masarepa is tender and the flavors meld.
Transfer the warm tagine onto a serving plate and sprinkle with chopped cilantro.
Garnish with a drizzle of olive oil and a squeeze of fresh lemon juice if desired.
Serve immediately as a brunch or lunch dish, pairing with crusty bread or a light salad.
Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 2 days.
